msl_source_provenance: byte-identical to compiler/codegen/metal_target.hexa::codegen_emit_metal_msl output (verified by hand-trace of _metal_emit_preamble + _metal_emit_kernel_signature('vec_add') + _metal_emit_vec_add_body); compiler not re-invoked because metal_target.hexa is not yet wired into self/codegen_c2.hexa dispatcher (RFC 075 §3: codegen-only, no @gpu_kernel target dispatch wiring)

method.rng: deterministic LCG (seed 0xC0FFEEDEADBEEF, 24-bit mantissa) -> [0,1) then *100-50 for [-50,50) range
method.reference: CPU host-side a[i]+b[i] in Swift Float (FP32)
method.rationale: Single FP32 add is exactly representable on both CPU (x86/arm64 IEEE 754) and Apple GPU FP32 SIMD ALU; no fma fusion possible (single add, not a*b+c), so byte-equality is the correct gate per @D g3

honest_scope.is: Apple M3 GPU executes the emitter's canonical MSL output byte-eq vs CPU reference for N=1024 FP32 vec-add
honest_scope.is_not: general MFunc -> MSL emission (vec-add shape only) · driver-wired @gpu_kernel dispatch (codegen-only artifact) · larger-shape numeric coverage (FMA, FP16, threadgroup) · M1/M2 family validation (M3 only this fire)
